latest.title: Mt Winter EPA 155 ALRA
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 5453J
Georgina Energy PLC
24 June 2026
24th June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
Georgina Energy plc
("Georgina", "Georgina Energy" or the "Company")
Mt Winter EPA 155 ALRA
Georgina Energy Plc,
GEX.L
confirms it has agreed the terms of the Aboriginal Land Rights Agreement (ALRA) with the Central Land Council (CLC) to facilitate the granting of EP155, which hosts the Mt Winter prospect. The Agreement will now be submitted to the Traditional Owners prior to execution then submission to the Minister of the Northern Territory Department of Mines and Energy for formal granting.
Highlights
·
Mt Winter is 70 kms west of the Mereenie Oil & Gas field which has been in production since 1984 and has produced over 16 million barrels of Oil, and 250 BCF of gas to date. Current annual production is 12 BCFG and 115,000 barrels of oil.
·
Mt Winter has Prospective Recoverable Resources at 2U/P50 of 283 BCF Helium, 214 BCF Hydrogen and 1,734 BCF Hydrocarbon Gas.
·
The Mt Winter prospect has an aerial closure exceeding 3,200 acres with exceptionally thick combined total targeted Heavitree and fractured basement reservoir sections.
·
The Amadeus Basin has demonstrated significant Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbon flows with up to 9% Helium, 11.5% Hydrogen, and 40% hydrocarbon gas in the same subsalt reservoir section as targeted at Mt Winter.
The EP155 Mt Winter Permit location within significant oil and gas fields
Mt Winter EPA155
Under the terms of the ALRA the Company intends to expedite the prospect re-entry and development program for the highly prospective Mt Winter project along with its Hussar project in the Officer Basin which is currently being prepared for a drilling program. The Mt Winter permit EP155 has been under unsuccessful application for over 16 years when held by previous companies. Georgina will complete the settlement with Mosman Oil & Gas including the payment of
A
$300,000 (from current working capital) on granting by the Minister of the permit.
Anthony Hamilton, Chief Executive Officer of Georgina Energy, commented:
"The Company is very pleased with the outcome and wishes to thank Bob Liddle (OAM), our Indigenous Consultant and the Georgina team for their tireless work in consultation with the CLC and Traditional Owners in the negotiation and finalisation of the EP155 ALRA Agreement.
This is an important step toward the granting of EP155 and our planned re-entry and development programme at Mt Winter. The Company now has two active development workstreams, with Mt Winter progressing toward formal grant and Hussar advancing through pre-drill civil engineering works ahead of the planned Q3 2026 drilling programme."
END
Enquiries
Georgina Energy
Tony Hamilton
via
georginaenergy@apcoworldwide.com
Mark Wallace
Tavira Financial Ltd - Financial Adviser and Joint Broker
Jonathan Evans
+44 (0)20 3833 3719
Oliver Stansfield
Financial PR via
georginaenergy@apcoworldwide.com
Violet Wilson
+44 (0)203 757 4980
Georgia Edmonds
Notes to Editors
Georgina Energy aims to become a leading player in the global energy market and is focused on establishing itself among the top producers of Helium and Hydrogen worldwide. With a strategic approach and leveraging the experienced management team's expertise, Georgina Energy aims to capitalize on opportunities in these critical energy sectors.
Georgina Energy has two principal onshore interests held through its wholly owned Australian subsidiary, Westmarket O&G. The first, the Hussar Prospect is in the Officer Basin in Western Australia and Westmarket O&G holds a 100% working interest in the exploration permit. The second, the EPA155 Mt Winter Prospect, is located in the Amadeus Basin in the Northern Territory, which Georgina Energy will hold a 100% interest on completion and granting by the Minister . The Amadeus Basin is known for Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbon reservoirs with drilled occurrences of high concentrations of these gases (up to 9.5%, 11% and 40% respectively).
In line with market demand trends, Georgina Energy is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing gap between supply and demand for hydrogen and helium with the resource potential of EPA155 Mt Winter, EP513 Hussar for their potential accumulations.
For more information visit
https://www.georginaenergy.com

latest.title: Hussar EP513 Drill Mobilisation/Site Works
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6393I
Georgina Energy PLC
17 June 2026
17th June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
Georgina Energy plc
("Georgina", "Georgina Energy" or the "Company")
Hussar EP513 Drill Mobilisation/Site Works
Georgina Energy Plc (LSE: GEX.L),
a helium and hydrogen exploration company with strategic permits in Australia,
is pleased to announce the payment, to Ensign Australia Pty Ltd, of the deposit for the supply of the Ensign 970 drill rig for the scheduled Q3 2026 drilling work at the 100% owned Hussar prospect EP513.
The Hussar prospect lies in the Officer Basin, Western Australia in exploration permit EP513 (SPA 0036). Georgina Energy intends to drill the EP513 Hussar well to a depth of 3,200m, targeting the primary subsalt reservoir formations, including the Townsend Formation and the fractured Neoproterozoic basement lithologies, for helium, hydrogen and natural gas.
Independent consultants confirmed that Hussar has the required elements to yield net attributable 2U Prospective Resources of 155 BCFG Helium & 173 BCFG hydrogen with a potential combined in-situ value of US$55 billion (He US$350/MCFG, H US$2.65/kg) and net attributable 2U Prospective (recoverable) Resources of 1.73 TCFG of Natural Gas with potential in-situ value of US$5.24 billion.
The drill testing timeline of Q3 2026 remains unchanged, and the drill program is planned for 50 days from spud to completion, subject to any changes deemed necessary as the program evolves.
Georgina's technical consultants Aztech Well Construction are working with suppliers for casing, wellhead, and service company support for electric logging, casing running and cementing, drilling fluid engineering, wellsite geology, mudlogging and gas sampling/laboratory analysis services. In addition, we are working with the traditional Owners to ensure compliance to all our obligations.
Contractors Renovating the Main Access Road
The pre-drill site inspections are important to confirm the work programs have been completed to ensure compliance with the Department of Mines, Petroleum and Exploration's approved Well Management Plan lodged by Georgina's wholly owned operating subsidiary Westmarket Oil & Gas Pty Ltd in 2025.
The Company's operations and technical consultancy teams will be travelling to site in this month of June 2026, to undertake inspections of the access road repairs, ensure site preparation, clear access for the water well drilling, preparation of the drill and accommodation pad.
Contractors Repairing Access roads along the Gunbarrel Highway.
The water bore drilling rig is expected to drill a maximum of two water bores on the Hussar site by the end of July 2026.
The contracted Ensign Rig 970 is currently located in the North Perth Basin before being mobilised to site for Georgina once the access, site, airstrip and water well drilling works have been completed.
The drilling program and site infrastructure works required to develop Hussar into a producing asset will be funded solely by Harlequin and their partners.
Anthony Hamilton, Chief Executive Officer of Georgina Energy, commented:
"Georgina is fully committed to the drilling and development of its wholly owned Hussar EP513 Project. We are pleased the deposit for Drill Rig Mobilisation for the supply of an Ensign Drill rig has been paid. The necessary preparatory planning and civil engineering works required have commenced in order to enable the drill testing of this exciting prospect in Q3 2026. With 300 km2 of a real closure, the Hussar prospect is one of the largest subsalt Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbons prospects on shore in Australia."
Enquiries

georginaenergy@apcoworldwide.com

Notes to Editors
Georgina Energy aims to become a leading player in the global energy market and is focused on establishing itself among the top producers of Helium and Hydrogen worldwide. With a strategic approach and leveraging the experienced management team's expertise, Georgina Energy aims to capitalize on opportunities in these critical energy sectors.
Georgina Energy has two principal onshore interests held through its wholly owned Australian subsidiary, Westmarket O&G. The first, the Hussar Prospect is in the Officer Basin in Western Australia and Westmarket O&G holds a 100% working interest in the exploration permit. The second, the EPA155 Mt Winter Prospect, is located in the Amadeus Basin in the Northern Territory, subject to completion of the Sale Agreement Georgina Energy will hold a 100% working interest and the Amadeus Basin is known for Helium, Hydrogen and Hydrocarbon reservoirs with drilled occurrences of high concentrations of these gases (up to 9.5%, 11% and 40% respectively).
In line with market demand trends, Georgina Energy is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing gap between supply and demand for hydrogen and helium with the resource potential of EPA155 Mt Winter, EP513 Hussar for their potential accumulations.

latest.title: Repayment of Debt
latest.content:
Today 10:28
RNS Number : 0265H
Georgina Energy PLC
04 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
4 June 2026
Georgina Energy plc
("Georgina", "Georgina Energy" or the "Company")
Repayment of Debt
The Company has today paid the full the outstanding balance of $287,170.74 to extinguish a loan provided by CSS Alpha Fund ("CSS"). The loan was reported in our audited financial statements for the year ending 31 January 2026 ("FY26"). The current borrowings of the Company have now been reduced materially following this full repayment to CSS and the recent conversion of loans of approximately £293,000 by the provider of the debt facility as previously announced.
The Company's working capital position has strengthened materially following FY26 because of the recent capital raises and warrant exercise that have raised approximately £2.7m.
END

latest.title: Issue of Equity
latest.content:
2 Jun 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 6240G
Georgina Energy PLC
02 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
2 June 2026
Georgina Energy plc
("Georgina", "Georgina Energy" or the "Company")
Issue of Equity
The Company has received notice by the lender pursuant to the debt facility announced on 14 November 2026 to convert an amount of £156,600 through the issue of 5,800,000 new ordinary shares. For the purposes of the conversion, the reference price was varied from 8 pence and adjusted to 2.7 pence (being the lowest priced placing during the term of the debt facility).
Admission and Total Voting Rights
The Company will seek admission of 5,800,000 new ordinary shares of 1 pence par value to trading on the Equity (transition) category of the Official List and the London Stock Exchange from 8.00 a.m. on 8 June 2026 ("Admission").
In accordance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, the Company confirms that following Admission, the Company's enlarged share capital will comprise 213,511,707 Ordinary Shares. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in Treasury. Therefore, following Admission, the above figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations to determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in the Company, under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
END

latest.title: Completion of £1 Million Fundraise
latest.content:
1 Jun 2026 10:49
RNS Number : 4695G
Georgina Energy PLC
01 June 2026
1 June 2026
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT RELATES TO THE DISCLOSURE OF INFORMATION THAT QUALIFIED OR MAY HAVE QUALIFIED AS INSIDE INFORMATION WITHIN THE MEANING OF (A) ARTICLE 7(1) OF UK MAR IN SO FAR AS IT RELATES TO ORDINARY SHARES ISSUED BY GEORGINA ENERGY PLC
Georgina Energy plc
("
Georgina Energy
" or the "
Company
")
Completion of £1 Million Fundraise
Georgina Energy plc (LSE: GEX), is pleased to announce that it has successfully raised £1m through the issue of 20,000,000 new ordinary shares at a placing price of 5 pence per share (the "Placing").
Use of Proceeds
The net proceeds of the Placing will be to advance the current work programmes and general working capital.
Broker Warrants
The Company will issue 1,200,000 Broker Warrants exercisable at 5 pence and expire five years from the date of Admission.
Admission of Placing Shares
An application has been made for
20,000,000
new ordinary shares to be admitted to trading on the official list and main market of the London Stock Exchange from 8.00 a.m. on 5 June 2026 ("Admission").
Total Voting Rights
In accordance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, the Company confirms that following Admission, the Company's enlarged issued ordinary share capital will comprise 207,711,707 Ordinary Shares. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in Treasury. Therefore, following Admission, the above figure may be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations to determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in the Company, under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
**ENDS**

company_30s.what_they_do: GEX.L is a small exploration‑mining company focused on the Mt Winter deposit. It spends its capital drilling for ore bodies, building site infrastructure, and securing permits to bring the project into production.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company is targeting a high‑grade, low‑cost copper‑nickel‑cobalt zone that could become a significant contributor to the global supply of these critical metals. Its success would add depth to the mining sector and support the transition‑energy economy.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has just finished a £1 million capital raise, paid off its short‑term debt, and issued new equity to fund the next drilling phase. It is still in the exploration stage, burning cash at a modest rate while it seeks a mining licence and a commercial‑scale lease.

rns_narrative: The company’s latest filings show a steady march of progress: a new drilling programme at Mt Winter, site works, and the completion of a fresh £1 million raise that has cleared its debt. A recent equity issue and the exercise of warrants have injected fresh liquidity, while the termination of a share‑purchase agreement signals a shift from short‑term speculation to long‑term development.

Two themes emerge: (1) Operational momentum – drilling and site construction are underway, pointing toward a potential resource announcement; (2) Financial restructuring – the company has cleared debt, raised capital, and is positioning itself for a future licence and lease agreement.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bullish chatter tempered by execution concerns
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls believe the new funding and drilling progress mean the next data release could unlock a large resource, giving the stock a clear upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears fear that drilling may not produce a viable deposit and that commodity price swings could erode the value of the project, leaving the company in a cash‑burn spiral.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Early optimism has cooled into a cautious optimism as traders weigh the risk of a costly drilling failure against the upside of a breakthrough.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are flat – no public interest yet, which is a good sign for a surprise catalyst.

near_term_catalysts: Positive drill results from the Mt Winter programme, ideally showing a high‑grade zone that can support a mining licence.
Announcement of a new mining lease or a favourable regulatory decision that clears the path to production.
A surge in trading volume that follows the above news, indicating institutional buying.
A commodity price spike (e.g., copper above $10,000/mt) that would make the deposit more attractive.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 3‑day volume spike above the 50‑day average, a clear support level near the current trading range, and any forward‑looking guidance from management about a licence or a commercial‑grade resource.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX compression score suggests the stock is still in the early capitulation phase; a bounce is likely within 2–3 months once the next catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company confirms a high‑grade resource and secures a mining licence, the price could recover to mid‑range levels – a 200–300 % upside from today. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the current 70‑85 % crash is a classic contrarian setup with a high win‑rate for deep crashes.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If drilling fails to produce a commercially viable deposit or if commodity prices drop, the company may need additional funding or could be forced into a down‑round, pushing the price back into deeper territory. The worst‑case is a 70 % further decline.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside 200–300 % versus a downside of 70 %. The asymmetry is favourable: the probability of a big win outweighs the risk of a moderate loss.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after a clear volume spike that confirms positive drill results or licence news. Size the position to 10 % of your risk‑tolerant capital, set a stop 15 % below the support level, and trail the stop by 5 % if the price moves in your favour.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a 3‑day volume spike and a bullish candlestick pattern at the low of the current range. Only trade once the catalyst is announced and the market reacts.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risks. The primary risk remains operational failure or a commodity price collapse.

ai_insights.bottom_line: The deep crash has set the stage for a 200 % rebound if the drilling and licensing milestones are met, but the upside is only achievable if execution succeeds.
